title: Check If Two String Arrays are Equivalent
description:
Given two string arrays word1 and word2, return true if the two arrays represent the same string, and false otherwise.
A string is represented by an array if the array elements concatenated in order forms the string.
Example 1:
Example 2:
Example 3:
Constraints:
constraints: word1[i] and word2[i] consist of lowercase letters.

examples.example_text:
Input: word1 = ["ab", "c"], word2 = ["a", "bc"]
Output: true
Explanation:
word1 represents string "ab" + "c" -> "abc"
word2 represents string "a" + "bc" -> "abc"
The strings are the same, so return true.

hints: Concatenate all strings in the first array into a single string in the given order, the same for the second array.
Both arrays represent the same string if and only if the generated strings are the same.
